sourceMovie.title: Assassination
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Assassination (2015) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Choi Dong-hoon's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Kingdom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Assassination clicked for you, The Kingdom (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Azumi
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Assassination clicked for you, Azumi (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Assassination?
faq.answer: Try The Kingdom, Azumi, War—each shares DNA with Assassination while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Assassination?
faq.answer: Assassination (2015) is best described as Action, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Assassination worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
